user_query:
Please create a 7-email lead nurture sequence for people who download our "2026 State of Operations Automation" report. Audience: operations managers and directors at mid-market manufacturing and logistics companies in North America. Primary goal: book a 30-minute demo of our workflow automation platform within 21 days; secondary goal: segment by role (Ops Manager vs. Ops Director) when appropriate in copy and CTAs. Read the following for context and guardrails:
- input/product_marketing_context.md
- input/lead_magnet_details.md
- input/audience_segments.json
- input/brand_voice.yaml

Deliverables:
1) Write a structured plan to output/sequence_plan.json with fields: sequence_name, trigger, goal, length, timing, exit_conditions; and an emails array of 7 objects with these fields per email: number, name, send, subject, preview, body {hook, context, value, cta, sign_off}, cta_text, cta_link, and optional segment_conditions. Constraints: keep each subject line between 40-60 characters; email 1 should send immediately; include one clearly labeled case study email; email 7 should be a direct demo ask with clear urgency.

2) Export individual email copy files under output/copy/ named E1_{short_slug}.md through E7_{short_slug}.md. Each .md file must include the same subject and preview as in the JSON, plus clearly labeled sections: Hook:, Context:, Value:, CTA:, Sign-off:. Use the brand voice from input/brand_voice.yaml.

3) Create a schedule table at output/schedule.tsv with the header exactly "email_number	day_offset	subject" and 7 rows matching the emails. Day offsets must be integers from 0 to 21, with 0 for the first email. Ensure the subjects in this TSV match exactly those in output/sequence_plan.json.

4) Create a simple metrics plan at output/metrics_plan.json with keys: overall_kpis (array of strings) and per_email (array of 7 objects each containing email_number and primary_kpi).

Make sure the plan incorporates insights from all input files and follows a value-before-ask approach across the sequence.
